compiled_state.title: merge-to-main.sh fails when main repo has pending rebase conflicts from other worktrees
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Problem

merge-to-main.sh consistently fails during _phase_sync when the main repo has local commits from prior worktree merges that conflict with origin/main during git pull --rebase. The script aborts with CONFLICT_DATA and cannot recover via --resume (same conflict repeats).

## Observed Behavior

During /dso:end-session for epic fbd9-ba78 (worktree-20260325-181052):

1. _phase_sync successfully merges origin/main into the worktree branch (line 876)
2. Script cds to main repo and runs git pull --rebase (line 941)
3. Rebase hits conflicts in .test-index and tests/skills/test_complexity_evaluator_agent.py from commit b8f64f7e (a different worktree's merged work)
4. _auto_resolve_archive_conflicts aborts because non-archive conflicts are present
5. Script exits with CONFLICT_DATA: phase=pull_rebase
6. --resume repeats the same failure (state file records sync as incomplete)
7. Clearing the state file and retrying also fails identically

The worktree branch itself is clean, pushed, and fully merged — the failure is entirely on the main repo side during its own git pull --rebase from origin.

## Root Cause

merge-to-main.sh assumes the main repo can cleanly git pull --rebase from origin. When multiple worktrees merge to main concurrently (or when a prior merge left main ahead of origin), the rebase can hit conflicts between different worktrees' changes. The script has no fallback for this case — it only auto-resolves ticket-data conflicts, not code conflicts on main.

## Impact

- Blocks /dso:end-session from completing the merge phase
- Worktree cannot be auto-cleaned by claude-safe (branch not ancestor of main)
- User must manually resolve conflicts on the main repo from a non-worktree terminal
- The workaround (create a PR instead) works but bypasses the merge-to-main workflow

## Proposed Fix

1. Before git pull --rebase on main, check if main is already up-to-date with origin/main (git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main main). If yes, skip the pull entirely.
2. If pull --rebase fails with non-ticket conflicts, fall back to git pull --no-rebase (merge strategy) which is less likely to conflict on linear histories.
3. If both fail, offer to create a PR as an automated fallback instead of just printing an error.
4. Consider whether git pull --rebase on main is necessary at all — if the worktree branch was already synced with origin/main in step 1, the main repo just needs to fast-forward merge the worktree branch.

## Reproduction

1. Have two worktrees merge to main sequentially, where the second merge includes changes to .test-index that overlap with the first
2. Push from main after the first merge but not the second
3. Run merge-to-main.sh from the second worktree — git pull --rebase on main will conflict

## Files
- plugins/dso/scripts/merge-to-main.sh (_phase_sync, lines 931-966)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=0.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: replaced unconditional git pull --rebase with ancestor-aware merge — ancestor guard skips no-op pulls, diverged path uses merge instead of rebase with ticket-data auto-resolution, and non-ticket merge conflicts gracefully skipped since worktree branch carries origin/main content.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
